title: Avatar: Fire and Ash Box Office Analysis
text: Avatar: Fire and Ash has demonstrated remarkable staying power, maintaining the #1 position for five consecutive weekends despite new competition. The film has accumulated over $370 million domestically and $1.3 billion globally. According to Gold Derby's projections for January 23-25, the film is expected to earn approximately $8 million, placing it in second position behind the newcomer Mercy. The sequel's durability is noteworthy. Despite facing challengers like 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple (which debuted with $12.5 million but failed to displace the Avatar film), James Cameron's film has proven resilient. Analysts suggest that if Mercy underperforms expectations, Avatar could potentially extend its reign further. The article notes that maintaining box office dominance through six weekends is consistent with the pattern established by previous Avatar films, indicating the franchise's proven ability to weather theatrical competition during traditionally slower seasons.
